title: C002: Add container image CI workflow
summary: All four external review comments from greptile-apps[bot] were correctly addressed: (1) fork PRs are rejected early with a clear ::error:: message, (2) the mutable version-only tag is restricted to main-only pushes preventing cross-branch clobbering, (3) the unused image-ref output was removed from build-service-images, (4) base images now receive an immutable :${{ github.sha }} tag alongside :latest for commit-to-image traceability. Documentation updated consistently. All 61 verification tests pass. YAML validates. No new bugs introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (61/61 in verify_build_images_workflow.sh)
Edge cases are covered (tag derivation, branch sanitization, patch count)
REQ-001 through REQ-009 have test coverage
New behaviors from review #2 fixes have dedicated tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (SPDX header, quoted on, SHA pinning)
Changes are minimal and focused on review feedback
No dead code or unused outputs remain
Comments explain non-obvious rationale (fork PR, version tag restriction)

security_checklist.item: Fork PR detection uses correct GHA context (github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name)
No expression injection in run: blocks (env vars used consistently)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (GHA ternary for version tag, conditional step for fork check)
Review patterns created to prevent recurrence of missed issues

fail_fast_checklist.item: Fork PRs fail fast before any build steps execute
Smoke test fails workflow on non-zero exit

defensive_checklist.item: Fork PR check prevents cryptic 403 errors mid-build
Version tag restricted to main prevents silent cross-branch clobbering
SHA tags on base images provide immutable audit trail

suggested_improvements: D4: Consider adding test functions to verify_build_images_workflow.sh for the three new behaviors: (1) test_fork_pr_rejection_step_exists — verify the 'Reject fork PRs' step exists in build-base-images with the correct if condition, (2) test_base_images_have_sha_tags — verify both python-base and venv-builder tags include ${{ github.sha }}, (3) test_version_tag_restricted_to_main — verify the version tag line in build-service-images contains the github.ref_name == 'main' conditional. These are low-risk since the behaviors are simple GHA expressions, but explicit tests prevent future regressions.
